detail.answer: Gujarat had the highest installed wind capacity in 2025, with 12,677.48 MW as of 31.03.2025 [2].

The top three Indian states by installed wind capacity as of 31.03.2025 are:  
1. Gujarat – 12 677.48 MW  
2. Tamil Nadu – 11 739.91 MW  
3. Karnataka –  7 351.10 MW [2]
India’s total installed wind-power capacity as of 31 March 2025 was 50 037.82 MW. [1][5]
As of 10.07.2025, there were 14 wind turbine manufacturers offering WEGs in India.[2]
GE’s contribution during 2024-25 was 929 MW. [2]
The largest wind energy generator rating installed in India is 5200 kW. [2]
NIWE has installed 996 wind monitoring masts. [6]
Electricity generation from wind energy in 2024-25 was 83,347 MU [1].
By March 2025, non-fossil sources accounted for just over 46% of India’s total installed power capacity of 470 GW. [1]
By 31 March 2025, India had realised 23.26% of its estimated wind potential at 150 m above ground level [2].

The State‐&‐Make table for active manufacturers (Table 16) shows Senvion’s entry for Madhya Pradesh as blank (no turbines recorded) [3]. For context, that same row lists 212 WEGs (535.600 MW) in Gujarat and 24 WEGs (64.800 MW) in Karnataka.
According to the state‐ & make‐wise cumulative installations as on 31.03.2025, Senvion has 212 wind electric generators (WEGs) installed in Gujarat [3].
Suzlon’s contribution during 2024-25 was 336 MW [4].

According to the MNRE/NIWE “Abstract of Wind Monitoring Stations in India (As on 31st July, 2025)”, Tamil Nadu has 101 wind-monitoring stations (masts) established; the table does not list how many of these are currently in operation for Tamil Nadu [5].
Suzlon installed 847 turbines in FY 2016-17 [3].

Suzlon installed 435 wind electric generators during FY 2015-16 (i.e. installations up to March 2016)[2]. The state-wise break-up for Suzlon’s FY 2015-16 installations is not given in the provided tables.
The provided documents do not include a year-wise breakdown of the number of WEGs installed by Siemense Gamesa for FY 2020-21. No such data appears in the passages supplied.
Pioneer Wincon had cumulatively installed 682 wind turbines up to March 2010. [3]
Inox Wind Ltd. installed 1 wind turbine (WEG) during FY 2022-23 [15].
GE added 928.800 MW of wind capacity in India during FY 2024-25. [11]
